metadata.task_description: 优化 ccc-state-controller 项目：1) 参考 tests/ 目录结构完善单元测试覆盖 2) 使用 ui-ux-pro-max 重构 packages/ccc-state-controller 面板UI

user_intent.goal: 优化 ccc-state-controller 项目，包含两个主要任务：1) 单元测试完善 2) UI/UX 重构
user_intent.scope: packages/ccc-state-controller (extension package with installer and panel UI)
key_constraints: 参考 packages/ccc-smart-component-manager 的实现模式
保持现有功能向后兼容

critical.rationale: Canonical reference for Cocos Creator Jest patterns - defines test structure, mock strategies, and quality criteria. All new unit tests must follow these established conventions.
Target for UI refactoring using ui-ux-pro-max. Uses Editor.Panel.extend() pattern with inline CSS/template. Contains state management, IPC communication, and button handlers.
Core installer business logic - exposes install/update/validate/rollback/getStatus/uninstall APIs. Target for unit test coverage expansion.
Central IPC message hub coordinating Editor API with installer module. Contains all message handlers (install, update, uninstall, get-status) and Editor.Ipc communication.

high.rationale: Existing integration test suite with scenario-based organization (Fresh Install, Update, Validation, Rollback, Edge Cases). Provides pattern for test structure and test context creation.
Test utility module providing createMockCore, createMockInstallation, file assertions, and version comparison helpers. Essential for writing new installer tests.
Reference implementation showing modern panel patterns: i18n integration, data manager, logger, SVG icons, modular imports. Key reference for panel refactoring.
Jest configuration for isolated test environment with moduleNameMapper for assets/* paths and setupFiles for Cocos environment mocking.

medium.rationale: Reference test showing mock-cc pattern with beforeAll global setup, jest.clearAllMocks in beforeEach, and comprehensive describe organization.
UI/UX design skill with 67 styles, 96 palettes, workflow for design system generation. Key reference for UI refactoring with Pre-Delivery Checklist.
Version management module for reading/writing version files and comparing semantic versions. Needs unit test coverage.
File copying module with backup/restore functionality. Needs unit test coverage.
Installation validation module checking required files and structure. Needs unit test coverage.

low.rationale: Full assembled panel from smart-component-manager for reference. Shows build output structure.
Test environment bootstrap for proto loading and cc.game initialization. Required setup for tests using Cocos runtime.

prioritized_context.sorting_rationale: Priority based on: (1) User goal alignment - testing guide is critical for test task, panel is critical for UI task; (2) Exploration critical files synthesis - files mentioned across multiple exploration angles ranked higher; (3) Dependency graph - core modules before dependent modules; (4) Role classification - modify_target and test_target before pattern_reference.

architecture_patterns: Test Isolation Pattern (tests/ directory with own node_modules)

cocos_creator.types: cc.Node and cc.Component from cc namespace

testing.describe_organization: 4-block structure: Lifecycle, UI Update, Event Handlers, Edge Cases

forbidden_patterns: Using emojis as UI icons (use SVG instead)
Mock after import (jest.mock must come before)
Testing mock itself instead of real logic
quality_gates: All tests must follow TESTING_GUIDE.md patterns
Panel UI must use Editor.Panel.extend pattern

external.usage: Test framework with mocks and assertions

conflict_detection.mitigation_strategy: Incremental refactoring with backward compatibility: (1) Add unit tests for installer modules without modifying existing integration tests; (2) Refactor panel UI preserving Editor.Panel.extend pattern; (3) Extract utilities into separate modules while keeping main API stable

summary.key_patterns: UI Component Test Pattern, Pure Logic Test Pattern, Cocos Creator Panel Pattern, UI Refactoring Pattern
summary.integration_points: tests/TESTING_GUIDE.md defines test patterns; panel/index.js uses Editor.Panel.extend; ui-ux-pro-max provides design system

summary.key_patterns: Extension IPC Pattern, Panel Extension Pattern, Test Isolation Pattern, Mock Creation Pattern
summary.integration_points: Editor.Ipc for main-to-panel communication, Editor.Panel.open for panel display, moduleNameMapper for test path resolution

summary.key_patterns: Jest with ts-jest for core tests, Custom Node.js runner for installer tests, Mock-cc pattern with beforeAll setup
summary.integration_points: jest.config.local.js moduleNameMapper, setup-cocos.ts proto bootstrap, test-helper.js mock creation utilities

critical_files.rationale: Canonical test patterns reference mentioned in all 3 exploration angles; defines mock setup, describe organization, quality assurance
UI refactoring target using Editor.Panel.extend pattern; 780 lines monolithic; contains state management, IPC handlers, button handlers
Core installer business logic with install/update/validate/rollback APIs; target for unit test coverage expansion
Existing integration test suite with scenario organization; provides pattern for test structure and test context creation

conflict_indicators.description: Panel UI uses inline CSS/template strings while ui-ux-pro-max provides modern design system guidelines - need to apply design system within Cocos constraints
Core library uses Jest with TypeScript while installer uses custom Node.js test runner - new panel tests need framework decision

clarification_needs.question: What is the scope of unit test coverage expansion for ccc-state-controller?
clarification_needs.context: The ccc-state-controller package currently has installer.test.js but may need additional unit tests for panel UI logic and installer modules.
options: Full coverage: Test all installer modules (file-copier, validator, version-manager) + panel logic
Critical path only: Test installer/index.js main functions + panel IPC handlers
Incremental: Add tests for untested modules as bugs are found

clarification_needs.question: Should the panel UI refactoring preserve the existing Editor.Panel.extend pattern or consider alternative approaches?
clarification_needs.context: The current panel uses Cocos Creator 2.x native pattern with inline CSS and template strings. ui-ux-pro-max provides modern design guidelines but the technical constraints require Cocos compatibility.
options: Preserve Editor.Panel.extend pattern, apply ui-ux-pro-max only to CSS styling and layout improvements
Refactor to use a build step (like ccc-smart-component-manager/panel/build.js) to separate logic from template
Minimal changes - only fix accessibility and contrast issues without structural refactoring

clarification_needs.question: Should panel UI tests use Jest with jsdom (like core tests) or a custom test runner (like installer tests)?
clarification_needs.context: The ccc-state-controller panel is a Cocos Creator extension panel with HTML/CSS UI. Core library tests use Jest+jsdom with mock-cc, while installer tests use a custom Node.js runner.
options: Jest with jsdom - reuse existing test infrastructure and patterns from core library tests
Custom Node.js runner - follow installer test pattern with custom assertions
Hybrid approach - Jest for unit logic, custom runner for integration tests

constraints.constraint: Must follow TESTING_GUIDE.md patterns for all new tests
Panel must use Editor.Panel.extend() pattern for Cocos Creator 2.x compatibility
UI refactoring must not break existing panel functionality
No emoji icons - use SVG only per ui-ux-pro-max guidelines

all_patterns.patterns: UI Component Test Pattern: Mock before import, create scene in beforeEach, node.active=false, addComponent instantiation, 4-block describe structure
Extension IPC Pattern: main.js exports messages object; panel communicates via Editor.Ipc.sendToMain/sendToPanel
Jest with mock-cc pattern: Global cc namespace mock in beforeAll, jest.clearAllMocks() in beforeEach, instance['privateMember'] access

all_integration_points.points: moduleNameMapper in jest.config.local.js maps assets/* to ../assets/$1
